LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. TRIABLE ISSUES OF MATERIAL FACT EXIST AS TO WHETHER THE SPILLED LIQUID AND CUP ON THE FLOOR WERE OBVIOUS AND EXISTED FOR A SUFFICIENT PERIOD OF TIME; WHAT IS THE INDUSTRY STANDARD FOR SLIP RESISTANCE; AND WHAT IS THE INDUSTRY STANDARD FOR INSPECTING FOOD COURTS AT LARGE AIRPORTS WITH A HIGH VOLUME OF FOOT TRAFFIC. PLAINTIFF PRESENTS CREDIBLE EVIDENCE THAT FOOD COURTS AT LARGE AIRPORTS SHOULD BE INSPECTED EVERY 15 MINUTES. DEFENDANT'S CUSTODIAN LAST INSPECTED THE AREA IN THE FOOD COURT WHERE PLAINTIFF FELL ALMOST THREE HOURS BEFORE PLAINTIFF'S FALL. IF THE CUP ON THE GROUND PRESENTED AN OBVIOUS RISK OF INJURY THAT PLAINTIFF SHOULD HAVE AVOIDED, THEN IT ALSO PRESENTED AN OBVIOUS RISK THAT DEFENDANTS NEEDED TO REMEDIATE. THAT DEFENDANTS ALLOWED VIDEO SURVEILLANCE OF THE AREA WHERE PLAINTIFF FELL TO BE DESTROYED PURSUANT TO AIRPORT POLICY DOES NOT SUPPORT THEIR MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976. (302/EG)
ORDER DENYING DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar May-26-2016 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jan-29-2016 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Jun-14-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jan-19-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court adopted its tenative ruling. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. Triable issues of material fact exist as to whether the spilled liquid and cup on the floor were obvious and existed for a sufficient period of time; what is the industry standard for slip resistance; and what is the industry standard for inspecting food courts at large airports with a high volume of foot traffic. Plaintiff presents credible evidence that food courts at large airports should be inspected every 15 minutes. Defendant's custodian last inspected the area in the food court where Plaintiff fell almost three hours before Plaintiff's fall. If the cup on the ground presented an obvious risk of injury that Plaintiff should have avoided, then it also presented an obvious risk that Defendants needed to remediate. That Defendants allowed video surveillance of the area where Plaintiff fell to be destroyed pursuant to airport policy does not support their motion for summary judgment. Order signed.

Judge: Ernest Goldsmith. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/EG)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-13-2015 Set For Trial On Feb-22-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206
